skill.name: medical-email-polisher
skill.description: Transforms rough email drafts into polished, professional medical correspondence using recipient-specific templates and tone-aware language refinement.
skill.summary: Polish medical emails with templates for mentors, editors, colleagues, and patients.
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: Static analysis detected 44 patterns across 4 files and assigned an initial risk score of 100/100. All 44 findings were evaluated and determined to be false positives: 'weak cryptographic algorithm' detections flagged normal documentation text with no cryptographic code present; 'system reconnaissance' detections flagged documentation about input parameters and validation; 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' detections flagged markdown backtick formatting characters used for code formatting, not shell execution; 'hardcoded URL' flagged a standard GitHub source attribution link; 'path traversal' detections flagged security checklist items that explicitly warn against path traversal. The actual executable code in scripts/main.py is a straightforward text-processing script with no network access, no file system writes, no credential handling, and no code injection vectors. The skill is a legitimate academic communication tool. Risk is rated low due to the presence of executable Python scripts and shell commands, which are inherent to the skill's documented functionality.

dangerous_patterns.title: Markdown Backtick Formatting Confused with Shell Execution
dangerous_patterns.description: The static scanner flagged 26 markdown inline code backtick characters across SKILL.md as Ruby/shell backtick execution. These are markdown formatting characters used for code spans and references, not executable backtick syntax. The only actual shell commands are in explicit fenced code blocks (lines 37-41, 64-66, 72-75) which are standard documentation for running the skill's Python script. All 26 detections are false positives.

dangerous_patterns.title: Security Checklist Warnings Flagged as Path Traversal
dangerous_patterns.description: The scanner detected '../' sequences on SKILL.md lines 125 and 128 and flagged them as path traversal. These lines are part of the skill's security checklist and explicitly warn against path traversal: 'No unauthorized file system access (../)' and 'Input file paths validated (no ../ traversal)'. The text is advising developers to prevent path traversal, not implementing it. False positive.

dangerous_patterns.title: Documentation Text Flagged as System Reconnaissance
dangerous_patterns.description: The scanner flagged documentation paragraphs describing input parameters, validation commands, and test cases as system reconnaissance across SKILL.md and the audit result JSON. These are normal documentation patterns describing what inputs the skill accepts and how to validate them. No reconnaissance behavior exists. All 8 detections are false positives.

dangerous_patterns.title: Normal Text Flagged as Weak Cryptographic Algorithm
dangerous_patterns.description: The scanner flagged 14 locations as containing weak cryptographic algorithms. The flagged lines include YAML frontmatter (SKILL.md:3), documentation text (SKILL.md:26,95), Python f-strings (scripts/main.py:91,93), and audit notes in JSON. No cryptographic code of any kind exists in this skill. All 14 detections are false positives with no identifiable basis for the detection.

dangerous_patterns.title: Source Attribution URL Flagged as Hardcoded Network URL
dangerous_patterns.description: The scanner detected a GitHub URL on SKILL.md line 7 and flagged it as a hardcoded network URL. The URL is https://github.com/aipoch/medical-research-skills used in a markdown blockquote for source attribution. The skill's Python code makes zero network requests. This is standard open-source attribution, not a security concern. False positive.

content.user_title: Polish Medical Emails to Professional Standards
content.value_statement: Rough medical email drafts risk miscommunication with mentors, editors, and patients. This skill transforms informal drafts into polished professional correspondence with recipient-specific templates and tone-aware language refinement.
actual_capabilities: Transforms rough email drafts into polished professional medical correspondence
Applies recipient-specific templates for mentors, editors, colleagues, and patients
Adds appropriate professional openings and closings automatically when missing
Improves grammar and clarity by replacing informal language with professional alternatives
Generates subject line suggestions based on email content keywords
Produces structured JSON output with polished email text, subject line, changes made, and tone assessment
limitations: Does not send emails directly. Only generates email text for manual review and sending
Requires user to specify recipient type for appropriate tone and template selection
Cannot verify medical accuracy or clinical appropriateness of email content
Template-based approach may not suit all communication styles or cultural contexts

use_cases.title: Academic Researcher
use_cases.description: Polish emails to journal editors, research mentors, and academic collaborators using formal academic tone and proper salutations.

use_cases.title: Healthcare Professional
use_cases.description: Refine patient communication with clear, warm, and professional language while maintaining HIPAA awareness in correspondence.

use_cases.title: Medical Administrator
use_cases.description: Standardize professional correspondence across teams with consistent formatting, tone, and structure for colleague communication.
use_cases.target_user: Medical office administrators and department coordinators

prompt_templates.title: Quick Polish for a Mentor
prompt_templates.prompt: Polish this email to my research mentor: "hey, wanted to ask about the manuscript revisions. thx"
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner user with a short informal draft needing basic polish

prompt_templates.title: Formal Editor Correspondence
prompt_templates.prompt: I need to email a journal editor about my submission status. Please polish this draft with formal academic tone: "hi, just checking on paper #2024-0156, been a while, wondering what's up"
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate user needing specific recipient type and formal tone

prompt_templates.title: Patient Communication with Context
prompt_templates.prompt: Polish this email for a patient about their recent lab results. Use warm but professional language suitable for patient communication. Draft: "your tests came back normal, let me know if you have questions"
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced user specifying recipient type, tone, and purpose

prompt_templates.title: Full Workflow with Output Review
prompt_templates.prompt: Run the complete medical-email-polisher workflow: polish this draft for a colleague, generate a subject line, list all changes made, assess the tone, and note any assumptions. Draft: "gonna need those reports by friday, pls send them over when you get a chance thx"
prompt_templates.scenario: Expert user requesting full structured output with detailed change tracking

output_examples.input: Polish this email to my mentor: "hey, wanted to follow up on our discussion about the manuscript"
output_examples.output:
Dear Dr. [Name],

Hello, I wanted to follow up on our discussion about the manuscript.

Thank you for your time and guidance.

Best regards,
[Your Name]

---
Subject: Follow-up: [Brief Description]
Tone: respectful and professional
Changes: Added professional opening, Added professional closing, Replaced informal greeting

output_examples.input: Polish this email to a colleague: "pls review the attached paper when you get a chance thx"
output_examples.output:
Hi [Name],

Please review the attached paper when you get a chance.

Thank you.

Looking forward to your thoughts.

Best,
[Your Name]

---
Subject: Request: [Brief Description]
Tone: professional but friendly
Changes: Replaced informal abbreviations, Added professional closing

output_examples.input: Polish this email to a patient: "your test results are ready, call the office to discuss"
output_examples.output:
Dear [Name],

Your test results are ready. Please call the office at your earliest convenience to discuss them.

Please don't hesitate to reach out if you have any questions.

Best regards,
[Your Name]

---
Subject: [Subject]: [Brief Description]
Tone: warm and clear
Changes: Added professional opening, Added professional closing, Improved clarity

best_practices: Always specify the recipient type to ensure the correct tone and template are applied
Review the generated email for medical accuracy and HIPAA compliance before sending
Use the subject line suggestion as a starting point and customize it for your specific context
anti_patterns: Do not rely on polished output for clinical decisions or medical advice without professional review
Do not include sensitive patient identifiers or protected health information in email drafts
Do not skip manual review. The tool improves language but cannot verify factual accuracy

faq.question: What types of email recipients does this skill support?
faq.answer: The skill supports four recipient types: mentors, journal editors, colleagues, and patients. Each has a tailored template with appropriate openings, closings, and tone.

faq.question: Does this skill send emails automatically?
faq.answer: No. The skill only generates polished email text. You must review the output and send it through your own email client.

faq.question: Is this skill HIPAA compliant?
faq.answer: The skill provides HIPAA-aware language patterns and communication guidance. It does not guarantee compliance. You must review all patient communication before sending.

faq.question: Can I customize the email templates?
faq.answer: Yes. Edit the TEMPLATES dictionary in scripts/main.py to adjust openings, closings, and tone descriptions for each recipient type.

faq.question: What Python version and dependencies are required?
faq.answer: Python 3.10 or higher. No additional third-party packages are required. The skill uses only the Python standard library.

faq.question: How does the skill handle informal language in drafts?
faq.answer: The clarity improvement step replaces informal words like 'hey', 'yeah', 'gonna', 'wanna', 'thx', and 'pls' with professional alternatives automatically.
